title: Kilo as a Systems-Programming Case Study
topic: Kilo as a systems-programming case study

concepts.title: Why build Kilo?
concepts.brief: Goal: explain the learning value of a tiny text editor and set expectations for scope. Length: 8-10 pages.

concepts.title: What a text editor must do
concepts.brief: Goal: introduce the core editor loop, buffer model, rendering, input, and persistence. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: C and terminal prerequisites
concepts.brief: Goal: cover the C concepts and terminal behavior needed to follow the implementation. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Raw mode and key input
concepts.brief: Goal: show how Kilo reads keystrokes directly from the terminal and why raw mode matters. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Escape sequences and screen drawing
concepts.brief: Goal: explain cursor movement, screen clearing, hiding the cursor, and full-screen redraws using VT100-style sequences. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: The refresh cycle
concepts.brief: Goal: build the mental model for how the editor repeatedly reads input and repaints the screen. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Editor state
concepts.brief: Goal: define the central state object and explain what must be tracked globally. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Text storage
concepts.brief: Goal: model lines, insertion points, horizontal tabs, and the relationship between file data and screen data. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Moving around the file
concepts.brief: Goal: implement cursor movement, scrolling, and viewport management. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Editing text
concepts.brief: Goal: cover insertion, deletion, newline handling, and merging/splitting rows. Length: 15-18 pages.

concepts.title: Opening files
concepts.brief: Goal: load text into memory and preserve line structure. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Saving files
concepts.brief: Goal: write edited content back to disk and explain dirty-state tracking. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Search
concepts.brief: Goal: implement incremental search and explain temporary UI modes and match navigation. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Syntax highlighting basics
concepts.brief: Goal: introduce token classification, line-level parsing, and color rendering. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Highlighting C code
concepts.brief: Goal: show how keywords, strings, comments, and numbers are identified in practice. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: File types and highlight rules
concepts.brief: Goal: make highlighting configurable and discuss how to extend it to more languages. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Error handling and cleanup
concepts.brief: Goal: restore terminal settings safely and exit cleanly on failure. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Portability and limitations
concepts.brief: Goal: explain POSIX assumptions, terminal dependencies, and what Kilo intentionally leaves out. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Refactoring Kilo
concepts.brief: Goal: turn the tutorial code into a cleaner architecture and discuss maintainability tradeoffs. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Adding features carefully
concepts.brief: Goal: explore line numbers, undo/redo, better search, clipboard support, and richer highlighting. Length: 12-15 pages.

concepts.title: Testing a terminal app
concepts.brief: Goal: separate editor logic from terminal I/O and design practical tests. Length: 10-12 pages.

concepts.title: Rebuilding the editor from memory
concepts.brief: Goal: recap the full architecture and help readers internalize the design. Length: 8-10 pages.

notes: not verbose, to the point. Reader is someone interested in developing useful softwares. reference links: https://github.com/antirez/kilo ; https://antirez.com/news/108
